title: claude-dotfiles — Machine Configuration
description: Machine-specific configuration. Maps canonical variable names to local paths on this machine. Never commit this file.

name.description: A short, memorable name for this machine.

os.description: Operating system. Determines which shell commands Claude generates.

home.description: Absolute path to the home directory on this machine.

project_root.description: Where code lives — git repositories, source files, compiled artefacts, and build outputs.

knowledge_root.description: Parent folder whose immediate subdirectories are all knowledge directories. All subdirectories are auto-discovered and named by their folder name. Combine with knowledge_dirs for any that sit outside this parent.

knowledge_dirs.description: Named knowledge directories. Each key becomes an uppercase variable in Claude sessions (e.g. 'notes' → $NOTES). Values are absolute paths on this machine. Use instead of, or alongside, knowledge_root.

command_prefix_enabled.description: Enable custom command dispatch. When true, Claude watches for messages starting with command_prefix and dispatches them to ~/.claude/commands/. Disabled by default — opt in deliberately.

command_prefix.description: The prefix that triggers a custom command. Any string except / (which the Claude Code CLI intercepts). Examples: --, !, >, cmd:, run:. Set to empty string "" for no prefix — command names are used directly (e.g. 'daily'). Only used when command_prefix_enabled is true.
